title: Alone in Beijing: How Chinese dissidents challenge the regime
subhed: 'Resisting a repressive regime' (2/3). Starting in 2020, China's zero-Covid policy and strict lockdowns triggered widespread protests, fueling a political awakening among young people. As the authorities tightened control, some have responded with passive resistance.

title: US strikes radar installations in Iran

Mali's ruling junta sentences a DGSE agent to 20 years in prison
subhed: Yann V., who was officially registered as an agent of France's foreign intelligence service (DGSE) with Malian authorities, was arrested in August 2025 in Bamako, accused of having attempted to orchestrate a 'destabilization attempt' against the junta led by General Assimi Goïta.

title: Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution' rises up against the Trump family and Prime Minister Edi Rama
subhed: Several thousand people have been protesting since May 30 against large-scale projects that many Albanians consider a symbol of the prime minister's excesses ever since he came to power in 2013.

title: Cobolli to face Zverev in French Open final after Arnaldi pulls out
subhed: Italian Matteo Arnaldi withdrew from the tournament because of illness, sending Flavio Cobolli into his first Grand Slam final.

title: Bold new proposal calls for merging Paris with suburbs
subhed: Clément Beaune, the French high commissioner for strategy and planning, proposed a sweeping overhaul of the Greater Paris area and its local governments.

Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
subhed: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.

title: How French courts became Europe's toughest on women returning from IS
subhed: Of the 23 terrorism trials scheduled in French courts in 2026, 18 involve women who joined the Islamic State organization in Syria. While their role was once overlooked, they are now being judged on equal footing with men.

title: Hungary's Magyar to Le Monde: 'I was not elected simply to change the government, but to change the regime'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, Viktor Orban's successor explains how he plans to recover the frozen €16 billion in European funds earmarked for Hungary. He insists that Budapest will not block the opening of EU accession talks for Ukraine, provided that Kyiv guarantees the rights of its Hungarian minority.
